title: The nightly platform run: six sources in, one dashboard green

steps.narration: 02:00. The NimbusMart platform DAG wakes on schedule. Nothing runs yet — the first row of tasks are sensors, each blocking on one upstream export landing in the drop zone. This is the shift supervisor reading the clipboard: no forklift moves until the trucks are confirmed at the dock.

02:04. All six exports have landed — the sensors clear one by one and release the DAG. The fraud_scores feed arrived last, as it always does (the scoring model runs after the orders close). A late or missing export here fails the sensor and pages on-call; tonight everything is on time.

state.note: sensors satisfied → the ingest layer is unblocked

steps.narration: Bronze ingest fans out — six parallel tasks, each an append that copies its source verbatim into the receiving dock. No typing, no cleaning, no rejects: Bronze's only contract is land everything, reject nothing, keep the raw shape so you can always replay. Row counts in, exactly as delivered.

state.note: Bronze = raw append · schema-on-read · order_events lands with its drift intact (17 rows missing device, 25 carrying a new app_version)

steps.narration: Silver's quality gates fire at the Bronze→Silver boundary. Rows are typed to the contract, deduplicated on their business key, and any row that fails an expectation is routed to quarantine, not dropped silently. Two gates bite tonight: 15 orders arrived with no fraud score, and 3 customers have a null city.

state.note: quarantine is a table, not a log line: an unscored order can't enter the review pipeline, but it's parked where an analyst can see and reprocess it

steps.narration: The SCD2 customer dimension rebuilds. Most customers are unchanged, but the customer_updates feed carries C-0042's move: Munich → Hamburg. The dimension closes the old row (sets its valid_to) and opens a new current row — two versions of one customer, so a May order still joins to Munich and a June order to Hamburg.

state.note: SCD2 = history without overwriting · updated_at Munich 06-05 → Hamburg 06-22 · surrogate key per version keeps every past order's context correct

steps.narration: Gold marts build on top of Silver — the showroom, arranged for the reader. The order fact joins to the customer and seller dimensions; a revenue-by-country rollup aggregates in one shuffle. FR leads at €22,655 across 58 orders, AU trails at €10,081. This is the tile the finance dashboard reads at 06:00.

The fraud-review-queue mart runs the join that the whole capstone points at: Silver orders joined to fraud_scores, filtered to fraud_score >= FRAUD_REVIEW_THRESHOLD (0.80), enriched with customer and seller context. The shuffle for this join is skewed — seller S-777 owns 80 of 240 orders, so its partition is the fat one every executor waits on.

state.note: the hot key from module E3 is still here in production — one seller's partition is ~3.6× the others; watch executor-3 carry it

steps.narration: The review-queue stage in flight: four executors, three chewing through balanced shards in a blink while executor-3 sits at 96% on the S-777 partition. The stage is only as fast as its slowest task — the classic skew tax. In production this is tolerated (a few extra seconds nightly); at 10× the data you'd salt it.

state.note: three executors idle, one still running — the shuffle put 33% of the rows behind a single task

steps.narration: The join lands and the filter fires: from 225 scored orders, exactly 43 clear the FRAUD_REVIEW_THRESHOLD (0.80) — 4 of them sitting precisely on the inclusive boundary. This is the same 43 the SQL acceptance test in H1 pinned as the contract, produced now by the full Bronze→Silver→Gold pipeline. The numbers reconcile.

state.note: gold.fraud_review_queue = 43 rows · 4 exactly on FRAUD_REVIEW_THRESHOLD (0.80) · S-777 alone contributes 19 of the 43

steps.narration: Every downstream task turns green. The DAG's leaf nodes — the dashboard refresh, the freshness check, the row-count reconciliation — run last, because the orchestrator only fires a task when all its parents succeeded. One red upstream would have short-circuited the whole subtree; tonight none did.

state.note: 21 tasks, 0 failures · the review-queue stage is the longest single hop, carrying the skewed join

steps.narration: 06:00. The executive dashboard lights up on schedule: revenue €114,847 across 240 orders, 43 orders in the fraud-review queue, 18 rows quarantined, freshness green. Every tile traces back through lineage to a Bronze source and a logical date — if finance asks “where did the 43 come from?”, the answer is one lineage click, not an afternoon.

state.note: dashboard: ● all green · review queue = 43 · Gold written as 14 right-sized files, ready for the morning read

steps.narration: That is the platform: six messy sources → Bronze append → Silver gates + quarantine → SCD2 + Gold marts → an orchestrated DAG → a dashboard that reconciles to 43. Every module you took is one task on this clipboard — schemas, joins, windows, skew, contracts, backfills, lineage. The capstone was never a new idea; it was all of them, wired together and run once, on time, at 02:00.

state.note: the whole course, one nightly run: ingest · quality · modeling · orchestration · observability — and the one number that proves it worked, FRAUD_REVIEW_THRESHOLD's 43-row queue, landing green
